compositional_truth_check.findings: P1 (Yellow Pot) contains String of Nickels. P3 (Black Pot) contains Pothos and White Rabbit anchor. P2/P4 (Black Pot) contains a single surviving leaf/sprout. No systemic loss of pots, but significant biomass reduction.

inventory_reconciliation.systemic_losses: None (all pots present), but high mortality of individual plant structures.

plant_audit.p1: String of Nickels: Stable, low-light adapted, minimal growth.
plant_audit.p2_p4: Mexican Mint/Silver Guest: High mortality. Only one primary leaf remains; previous stems have shriveled or detached.
plant_audit.p3: Pothos: One leaf remains, showing signs of chlorosis/necrosis at the margins. The rabbit anchor remains stable.

biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of white perlite/debris on soil surface; no fungal blooms detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Initial state shows significant yellowing of the Pothos leaf.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-1: Gradual decline in Pothos leaf turgor; P2/P4 shows progressive loss of secondary stems.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Stasis. No new growth; current state represents a 'Rested State' of decline.

anomalies: Loss of structural integrity in P2/P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of physiological stress. The Pothos (P3) is exhibiting signs of senescence or nutrient deficiency, evidenced by the persistent yellowing of the leaf. The Mexican Mint/Silver Guest (P2/P4) has suffered a catastrophic loss of biomass, leaving only a single, fragile sprout. The String of Nickels (P1) remains the most resilient, though it shows no signs of active expansion. The environment is stable but clearly insufficient for the current plant population's health requirements.
